news.description:
Hello everyone,

A new small patch has been released.

This one contains mostly fixes for regressions introduced by the November Update, as well as some other minor fixes.

Sandbox servers and clients are recommended to update at their earliest convenience.

The change list is as follows:

  • Fixed decals not rendering in Hammer

  • Re-added model scale to prop_dynamic in Hammer

  • Limit physics rescaling to prop_physics and sent_anim

  • Fixed server Lua error when using "" for rope material

  • Make Resistance Mine have proper name in killfeed

  • Added Cable_DX8 to rope shader whitelist which is used by some default materials

  • Combine Mine relationship adjustments

    • Respects Ignore Player and Disable AI settings

    • Doesn't attack Combine Gunship, Manhacks, Stalkers

    • Always attacks Barnacles and Xen Aliens from Half-Life 1

  • Fixed client D.O.G. ragdolls leaving behind ropes on removal

  • Set default m_flUseLookAtAngle for func_button to 0.8 (was 0) in engine

  • Set min_use_angle to 0.8 (was 0) for func_button in Hammer

  • Fixed certain NPCs (such as Alyx and Citizens) firing 2 bullets per pistol shot

  • Fixed Alyx firing the .357 Magnum also spawning reload shells

  • Fixed crashing on loading a Source Engine saves when Nextbots are present in the save file

  • Fixed map load crashes to do with corrupted maps (corrupted overlays)

  • Fixed prop gibs stopping spawning after the 300th in multiplayer

  • Fixed func_proprrespawnzone not being tracked properly clientside

  • Prevent recreating tool GhostEntity every tick with some tools (Community Contribution)

  • Fixed Motor tool torque axis being wrong if the motor was rotated after creation and before first activation (Community Contribution)

  • Fixed Lua errors when trying to open spawnmenu in Sandbox derived gamemodes with Spawnmenu disabled

  • Fixed main menu Lua errors when starting certain maps

  • Reimplemented kickid console command from scratch to hopefully resolve corrupted kick reasons issue

  • Reworked internals of Player:Kick and game.KickID to be consistent with each other

  • Fixed a regression with CBasePlayer::Kick that resulted in weird kick reasons

  • Fixed floating addons failing to mount on Linux systems

  • Don't try to load save files from mountable games

  • Fixed a regression with Entity:CreateParticleEffect when using tables

  • Fixed models/cs_fix.mdl not loading due to it being corrupted

  • Updated Zombine and Combine Soldier without armor player models to fix their animations

The entire change list can also be found on our website:

https://gmod.facepunch.com/news/december-2025-patch
news.title: December 2025 Patch

news.description:
Sonic Generations Delisting FAQ

Q: Can I still purchase the game at all after September 9th?
A: On September 9, the original version of Sonic Generations will no longer be sold on Steam as a standalone title. However, we’ve heard player’s feedback and we’re offering a way to play the game in its original form. The original version of Sonic Generations will still be available as part of the new Sonic the Hedgehog Legacy Bundle, including other classic Sonic titles on Steam.

Q: If I had already previously purchased Sonic Generations, will I still have access to the game when it gets delisted on September 9th?
A: Yes. Players who already own the original version of Sonic Generations will still be able to download and play the game.

Q: Will I still be able to mod Sonic Generations after it has been removed?
A: Yes. Players who already own the original version of Sonic Generations will still be able to mod the game, and all pre-existing mods will still work.

Q: What is included in the “Sonic the Hedgehog Legacy Bundle” that will be offered?*
A: The Sonic the Hedgehog Legacy Bundle will include:
o Sonic Generations Collection
o Sonic Adventure DX
o Sonic Adventure 2
o Sonic Adventure 2 Battle
o Sonic the Hedgehog 4 Episode 1
o Sonic the Hedgehog 4 Episode 2
o Sonic 3D Blast
o Sonic Spinball

Q: What is the price of the new bundle?*
A: The price of the new bundle is $59.99 USD.

*Bundle content and pricing may vary depending on region.
news.title: Sonic Generations - Delisting FAQ

news.description:
This Week in RaceRoom

January 12 to January 19

A new week means a fresh lineup in Ranked Multiplayer, a new Free2Play combo, and plenty of racing across sprint, feature, and weekly formats. Whether you’re chasing rating or just jumping in for some quick action, this week is stacked.

Free2Play Combo

Mercedes-AMG GT3 at Dubai Autodrome

The Mercedes-AMG GT3 goes Free2Play this week at Dubai Autodrome. Jump into one of the most iconic modern GT3 cars and take on the flowing, high-speed layout of Dubai. Free to play all week, perfect for new drivers and a strong option for ranked regulars looking for clean, competitive racing.

New Ranked Schedule

The ranked rotation has been refreshed with a new mix of Daily Sprint Races, longer Feature events, and full-length Weekly races. Expect a broad spread of cars and tracks, from short sprint formats to endurance-style challenges. All combos are live now.

Daily Sprint Races (15 min)

  • Super Touring at Brands Hatch GP

  • DTM 1995 at Salzburgring

  • GT3 at Dubai Autodrome

  • MX-5 at Mid-Ohio

  • F4 at Suzuka

  • TCR at Sepang

Daily Feature Races (~30 min)

  • Super Touring at Interlagos

  • GT4 at Nordschleife NLS

Weekly Races (45–60 min)

  • Friday: 992 Cup + GT4 at Dubai Autodrome

  • Saturday: LMDh + GT3 at Watkins Glen

  • Sunday: GT4 at Daytona

Online Racing Pack

The Online Racing Pack is the fastest way into competitive RaceRoom multiplayer. With a hand-picked selection of essential cars and tracks used across Ranked races, it gives you instant access to the combos that matter most. Jump straight into Daily Sprints, Feature races, and Weekly events without worrying about missing content, all at a much better value than buying items individually. If you want clean battles, full grids, and maximum racing time, this pack is the smart entry point.
news.title: This Week in RaceRoom

news.description:
Hello Getting Over it Fans

I wanted to let you know that the game we've been working on since 2019 is finally out - Baby Steps, a challenging game about hiking with a lot of same flavor as Getting Over It.

The basic gameplay idea is that you walk up a mountain - only, you control your feet manually using the mouse buttons or the triggers on a controller. We were able to create some truly diabolical level design challenges within that system, and they're all hidden within a really large expansive open world.

I'm also back in the saddle as a voice actor for this one - in fact, I'm voicing seven different characters in the game across over 90 minutes of cutscenes. You can skip them of course but... even that may turn out to be harder than you think.

This has been a real labor of love for me and my teammates Gabe and Maxi, but I hope you will enjoy playing it as much as we have over the years.

You can get it on steam here:

Buy it now

Thanks as always for your support!

Bennett
news.title: Baby Steps is Out!

news.description:
We have released a hotfix that addresses a security-related concern raised to us by Unity. You can read more about the specific issue here: https://unity.com/security/sept-2025-01/

While the update patches out the flaw, Unity has said that, to their knowledge, no one has exploited the issue to date, and no players have been at risk.

To help ensure that this security flaw can never be exploited on Subnautica or Below Zero, we have updated Unity following the guidelines provided. We have also had to make the difficult decision to disable all legacy and alternative branches of Subnautica and Below Zero, as there is no way for us to patch out the security issue on each of those branches individually.

If you play Subnautica or Below Zero with any mods on our legacy or alternative branches, you should disable or remove them before launching the game after the update to preserve your save files. You will need to check that the mods you want to play are updated to work with the most recent version of the game.

For our speedrunning community, this also means that the speedrunners branch has been disabled. We understand that this will affect speedruns moving forward, but the security and safety of all our players is our highest priority.

Thank you for all your continued support for Subnautica and Below Zero!

Keep Diving.
news.title: Subnautica Security Hotfix

Start the year off right by keeping your nose clean while bagging laundered GTA$ through a sophisticated network of legit businesses. Play GTA Online anytime through January 14 to claim the Hands On Car Wash for free from Maze Bank Foreclosures — the gateway to a portfolio of Money Fronts. To unlock your free Hands On Car Wash property, just make sure you've become acquainted with Raf and Mr. Faber by playing through the Money Fronts introductory cutscene.

Get back to business throughout January with a collection of special bonuses for enterprising GTA Online players, culminating in the arrival of three new Odd Jobs later this month.



3X GTA$ AND RP ON LEGAL FRONTS

Dispel the heat surrounding your illicit activities by keeping up appearances at your Legal Fronts and earn 3X GTA$ and RP for washing cars via the Hands On Car Wash, giving sightseers helicopter tours at Higgins Helitours, and delivering organic product for Smoke on the Water.

Complete three Car Wash jobs to fulfill the Weekly Challenge and earn a bonus GTA$100,000.

On the less-than-legitimate side, Counterfeit Cash Factory owners can earn 2X GTA$ for selling Counterfeit Cash from their Biker Business or Nightclub this week. Uninitiated forgers can take advantage of 40% off Counterfeit Cash Factory properties and their Upgrades and Modifications via The Open Road (accessible from the laptop inside your MC Clubhouse).

OPERATION PAPER TRAIL BONUSES

Become a deputized agent of the Internal Affairs Agency and get to the bottom of the state’s gas price crisis in Operation Paper Trail, paying out Double GTA$ and RP.

Report to the U icon in Downtown Los Santos for your briefing.

See full event details at the Rockstar Newswire.

news.description:
Gang Beasts patch 1.28 comes out today for currently supported platforms. The 1.28 patch addresses a recently disclosed security vulnerability that can compromise the security of games made with specific versions of the Unity editor. Unity has published resources for patching versions of the Unity editor with this vulnerability (this includes Unity 2021.3.33f1, the build of Unity that Gang Beasts patch 1.27 was made with).

To remove the potential for this vulnerability to be exploited it is important to delete older versions of Gang Beasts and install patch 1.28 as soon as you can.

Gang Beasts 1.28 also makes back-flipping less difficult compared with patch 1.27.

The 1.28 release does not include new stages or costumes or other content and modifications but we plan to give more information on development this month.

The key modifications and fixes made in the 1.28 build are:

  • implemented patch for Unity security vulnerability

  • modified character physics to make backflips less difficult
news.title: Security Advisory (patch 1.28)

news.description:
Soon we'll be making some changes in order to support cross-platform cloud saves between Windows, MacOS, and Linux (including the Steam Deck)!

This is planned to occur on Monday, 15th of August at 05:00 (UTC).

UPDATE: These changes have been applied. Please take care to backup your save files from the locations below before attempting to sync with Steam Cloud. In order for your save files to sync properly you may need to open them up in-game once and then save and quit, so Steam triggers the upload.

These changes will replace the MacOS and Linux cloud save locations, and all platforms will use the existing Windows save location. This will have a few side effects:

  • MacOS and Linux users will need to play the game once for Steam to upload their existing local save files to cloud storage
  • Windows users should not need to do anything, except:
  • Cross-platform users who manually migrated their save files between platforms may have conflicting files. When steam prompts you to keep or overwrite your files it is important to choose the correct one carefully (which will depend on your own situation). If in doubt, back up your save files first!

Save File locations

Windows:
%USERPROFILE%/AppData/LocalLow/Team Cherry/Hollow Knight
MacOS:
~/Library/Application Support/unity.Team Cherry.Hollow Knight
Linux:
~/.config/unity3d/Team Cherry/Hollow Knight

Help: missing save files

In some edge cases it may appear that your save files are missing. First, confirm that they can't be found at the locations above. You can manually download save files from the Steam Cloud website and place them in their relevant locations.
news.title: Cross-platform Cloud Saves

application.name: Scrap Mechanic
application.description: A building game about a stranded mechanic on a farming moon.

news.description:
Hi Mechanics!
It’s good to be back with another devblog!
Where should we start? Let’s address the big question first!

Where is the next chapter?

Believe it or not, it is coming along, but it hasn’t been without challenges. Earlier this year, we ran into some significant design and technical issues with the underground digging gameplay. Simply put, it wasn’t meeting the quality bar we expect from Scrap Mechanic.

Once we reached a version that felt right from a gameplay perspective, it also required a heavy optimization pass to make sure it performs well in a large underground world. That meant more iteration and testing than originally planned.

We could have made development easier for ourselves by lowering the bar or simplifying parts of the feature, but that’s not how we approach Scrap Mechanic. We care deeply about delivering something that feels fresh, solid, and worth the wait, even when it means taking longer. We’re now genuinely happy with where it’s headed, and we believe that will be clear when you finally get to play it. 

So when is it coming out?!

It’s not this year, unfortunately. But we are very sure you will finally get to play the next chapter next year! The release date will be announced in the coming months, as well as the name! That’s all we can say right now.

Let’s jump into some new stuff!

We noticed that we had enough spoiler-free content to make a new devblog.

So here goes!

New Survival Terrain

The survival terrain has received a really big overhaul, and we finally got around to adding all the things that we always wanted for the generated survival world. The aim was to create more varied and visually interesting places: beautiful forest roads as well as bridges, new mountain formations, and better desert biomes. We also added new ruins, secrets, as well as making sure road systems are better and easier to navigate with improved linking. Here are some new shots from the new survival terrain.




Raid Redesign

Another part of survival that got a very big overhaul is the raids! We have turned it into a much more well-designed game element with a lot more depth than what’s currently in survival. The difficulty scales a lot better now. Enemies are a lot smarter, choosing better paths. We redesigned the raid UI and made the raids start right away when they get triggered instead of making Mechanics wait for a day. They are much more engaging and fun now, including a lot of our newer bots as well.



The Cornade

We are excited about this one!

This is a new weapon in Scrap Mechanic called the Cornade. We noticed that some players ended up in really tricky situations in the warehouse and decided to add a grenade-type weapon to help in these certain unfair enemy encounters. Now that we’ve added it, we can’t understand why we didn’t do it sooner. We also made it super satisfying to use—simple, but a great addition to Scrap Mechanic.




Multishape Parts

Something that was always a bit of a problem with the building parts in Scrap Mechanic is that parts could not have multiple shapes within a single part. This is now resolved, and with that we’ve added a few new parts that have this new possibility. It’s a bit of a niche thing, but we want to have it included since it offers quite a few new and fun ways to interact with parts.



Useful Ladder

Ever wanted to build a fire truck with a folding ladder? Maybe just have a ladder to keep your creations tighter without the need for a big staircase? Well, we added a ladder. Since Scrap Mechanic is a very creative and free game, we had to make sure the new ladder works on moving vehicles but also is possible to climb at angles you would expect to climb. Is it more superior than the toilet seat? You be the judge.

Freezer and Ice Blocks

The ice block is a very low friction block, making the Mechanic slide on top of it. Together with slants, it’s possible to build slides among other things. To craft the ice block, the player has to obtain a freezer and add water! Using this block in creative ways can be very beneficial. Especially with transportation systems.

Duckie Outfit

As a returning devblog tradition, we have to include a new outfit. This time, it’s time to reveal the Duckie outfit. It was on our drawing board for a very long time, and we finally got it made. Maybe one of our more wacky outfits, and really fun to mix with other outfits.


Updated Trashbot

Some of you Mechanics who have been following previous devblogs might remember the stinky Trashbot. We iterated on him quite a bit after that. We ended up doing a lot of changes and improvements. He is now a lot more intense than what we started with, but also way more fun to fight.

There is a lot more content that we’ve been working on, but that is saved for the release.

We wish you an amazing holiday and a happy new year!
Please keep a close eye on our channels in the coming months.

Stay creative, Mechanics!
news.title: Devblog #26

news.description:
Portal: Revolution, from the start, has included a native Linux build. As some players have recently reported, it was broken because of recent Steam updates. To fix this we have decided to remove the native Linux binaries in favour of Proton. Don't panic.

Our native Linux build sucked. You may have experienced frequent hangs and stutters related to gel rendering or other strange bugs. This stems from a bug in our multithreaded rendering code for gel blobs, and despite our best efforts we were not able to fix it. To make matters worse, it only happens on the native Linux build. Windows and Proton are not affected.

Because of these two reasons we'll ditch the native build and run the game through Proton. Performance should be comparable, if not even better at times and stability is definitely improved. This change shouldn't affect your gameplay in any way, but if you experience issues, please feel free to report them to our support email support@secondfacesoftware.com AFTER you have tried these troubleshooting steps:

Troubleshooting

These issues would normally not occur on a fresh launch but removing a native Linux build might catch Steam off guard. Here's some troubleshooting steps for issues we've encountered so far

"Missing executable ... revolution.exe"

Try restarting Steam or Verifying integrity of game files. If that doesn't help, set the game to use Proton version 9 (Or whichever works for you) in the Compatibility settings. Check the file path manually to see if that exe file exists.

Game doesn't start / Short black screen on Steam Deck (We're working on this)

Try setting the game to use Proton version 9 (Or whichever works for you) in the Compatibility settings

There might be some points during the transition, but I hope we can smooth it all out in time. I wanted to post this update early while we're still ironing out some kinks.

Stefan
news.title: Portal: Revolution on Linux

Play Now

Say, have you heard? Hear Say is here! 

We’re all done baking up Cookie Haus after a year in the oven!

Your suspicions  about our new social deduction game Suspectives coming out soon were correct - it’s here now! 

And if you’ve been ruminating on ways to ruin perfectly nice things, Doominate is here to fill that void. 

The legends have come to pass: Legends of Trivia is real and it’s ready to escort you and your friends on your trivia travels.

We’re also introducing the Big Bang Bundle: a combo of games that includes Survey Scramble and The Jackbox Party Pack 11 in one convenient constellation to keep the party going even longer!

Here’s what’s included in The Jackbox Party Pack 11:

  • Doominate (Writing): Take a perfectly lovely situation and destroy it in this head-to-head joke game where it feels so good to be so bad. Doominate supports 3-8 players.

  • Hear Say (Sound Effects): Record sound effects and dialogue directly from your phone in a foley-inspired game where laughter is the soundtrack. Then, watch your voices come to life in a series of short movie clips! Hear Say supports 2-8 players. 

  • Cookie Haus (Drawing): Play our coziest and kookiest drawing game yet by fulfilling cookie design orders for unusual clientele. Don’t play on an empty stomach! Cookie Haus supports 3-8 players.

  • Suspectives (Social Deduction): There’s been a crime, one player secretly did it, and the evidence comes from your own survey answers! Study the clues, defend your innocence (even if you’re lying), and put your friends on trial. Suspectives supports 4-8 players.

  • Legends of Trivia (Trivia): Assemble your team and put your trivia knowledge to the test in a fantastical world full of danger! Trivia! Teamwork! Monsters! Do you dare? Legends of Trivia supports 1-6 players.

You can find these games as part of The Jackbox Party Pack 11 now on all major platforms including Steam, Nintendo Switch, Xbox, and PlayStation. Also available on Apple TV & iPad, Amazon Luna, and most major Steam key sellers.

news.description:
We have released a hotfix today that addresses a security-related concern raised to us by Unity. You can read more about the specific issue here: https://unity.com/security/sept-2025-01/

While the update patches out the flaw, Unity has said that, to their knowledge, no one has exploited the issue to date, and no players have been at risk.

To help ensure that this security flaw can never be exploited on Subnautica or Below Zero, we have updated Unity following the guidelines provided. We have also had to make the difficult decision to disable all legacy and alternative branches of Subnautica and Below Zero, as there is no way for us to patch out the security issue on each of those branches individually.

If you play Subnautica or Below Zero with any mods on our legacy or alternative branches, you should disable or remove them before launching the game after the update to preserve your save files. You will need to check that the mods you want to play are updated to work with the most recent version of the game.

For our speedrunning community, this also means that the speedrunners branch has been disabled. We understand that this will affect speedruns moving forward, but the security and safety of all our players is our highest priority.

Thank you for all your continued support for Subnautica and Below Zero!

Keep Diving.
news.title: Below Zero Security Hotfix

news.description:
CREWMATESSSS!!!

WE MADE IT! WE’RE ALIVE! WE SURVIVED THE IMPOSTORS FOR ANOTHER YEAR!!!

Thank you for a ✨stellar 2025✨ full of fun updates, multiple vent cleaning claims, and you-definitely-not-asking-us-about-the-TV-show-and-where-it-is. (It is NOT cancelled??? Why do I keep seeing people claim this please lmao.)

I wanted to do a silly recap of what we’ve accomplished together this year, but FIRST let’s get through some housekeeping.

New Years Eve 2026 Drop

Our yearly tradition is coming! Get a special, limited time NYE 2026 Hat for FREE when you log in starting December 26, 07:00 UTC. Ring in 2026 with your Crewmates and ensure you can be the person in future lobbies going “Oh this hat? It was limited edition. You just had to be there.” Ahhh, FOMO. This giveaway ends on January 31, 2026 so don’t forget about it!

Until then, the Snowflake, Snowbean, and Gift Cosmicubes are BACK! Celebrate the winter holidays and snag them before they melt away on December 31.

Innersloth Holiday

Innersloth (that’s us) will be on winter holidays starting now (December 15) until January 5th, 2026. During that time expect less posts on social media (unless I get bored waiting in line for something and start replying to stuff on my phone lol) and no updates during this time. (Victoria not using parentheses while writing a devlog challenge: IMPOSSIBLE.)

If you encounter any bugs or issues with the game during this time, our player support team will still be helping out! You can email them here or check out the helpdesk.

2025 Recap

Now let’s take a walk down memory lane and see all the horrors tasks we’ve completed this year. Fun fact: this year, everyone on the Innersloth team are confirmed Crewmates. We think. We’re still not really sure about Gary.

First, let’s reflect on the 2025 Roadmap we released:

Improved Matchmaking

Update v16.0.0 back in March brought an important change to Among Us – a new way to create and find lobbies! We got solid feedback from you on what you needed to find your perfect match (thanks to everyone who filled out the Player Feedback form near the beginning of the year), so we added filters, the ability to view settings before you join a game, and more.

I am still obsessed with the way Crewmates fill up the maps on the Create Game screen by the way. The details of what they’re doing… *chef’s kiss*

New Roles

In September, we released two new roles in v17.0.0: Detective and Viper!

The Detective was our first Crewmate role that needed much more involvement and big brain IQ to properly strategize with. (Confession: I am terrible at this role. My brain is too small.)

Meanwhile, the Viper got a cool custom kill animation and got to wreak havoc in lobbies. Shout out to this one comment I saw that said “I got puked on by Viper, the animation is grosssss 😂”. Please consider it an honor to be puked on by Viper.

Collabs

The most recent (and ongoing) collab was with the ConcernedApe himself for a Stardew Valley Cosmicube! This all started when we bullied him into going to the aquarium with us in Seattle back in 2023. (I’m joking.) (But we did go to the aquarium!) Don’t forget to pick it up for a mere 3300 Beans before it ends in February 2026.

Our big collab this year was with RGG Studios to bring the Kamurocho gang to the game in an official Cosmicube. Please tell me you saw the karaoke video we made for it. Please. And if you already saw it, watch it again.

But that wasn’t all! To celebrate their Version Luna I launch, we’ve teamed up with Genshin Impact to bring a Paimon look to Among Us. The Impostors definitely used it as Emergency Food.

And back by popular demand was the Pusheen Cosmicube! Who knew something so cute could be so evil. You all truly have yelled at us SO MUCH to bring it back, and I have definitely seen a significant increase in Pusheens running around these lobbies, knocking over juice boxes.

If you had a keen eye (visor?) you also likely saw our Crewmates (Impostors?) popping up in quite a few other games too! Not only was the Among Us Backbling back in Fortnite, but you could see Crewmates invading What The Car?, Unrailed 2, Tavern Talk, Spirit City: Lo Fi Sessions, and Snake.io.

Cosmetics

We released a whole Racing Cosmicube in June! BEAN FOR SPEED. The team was making Fast & Furious jokes the entire month. (It’s not a Fast & Furious collaboration at all but it was too funny making “family” jokes.)

Security Stuff

We had depreciated 2022 and 2023 versions of Among Us to ensure everyone was on similar versions and got all the improved features, bug updates, and improved security measures.

Your safety is of utmost importance to us here! ❤ Trust that even if you don’t always hear it, we’re keeping an eye on trouble and doing our best to navigate legal matters, technical requirements, crossplatform measures, and implement safety features to make your time on Among Us as smooth as possible. (Safety from Impostor murders not guaranteed.)

TV Show Work

SWEATS NERVOUSLYHELLO…. THE SHOW IS STILL HAPPENING. NEWS WILL COME AT SOME POINT. I AM BEING CHAINED TO SECRECY. THE IMPOSTOR IS BEHIND ME WITH A KNIFE.

Aaaand that’s it! Huge thanks to you all for supporting us, playing games, and doing all your tasks this year. The fact we’ve been able to keep working on Among Us after all these years is crazy – it’s not often this happens, especially in the indie game space.

We’re grateful! Can’t wait to do 2026 chaos together!

Victoria and the Innersloth Team
news.title: Happy End of 2025, Crewmates! 🎉 Year End Recap

news.description:
Hello everyone!

I'm happy to announce that version 0.13 of Baldi's Basics Plus is now available!

My apologies for the delays on this one. The new feature, stickers, which I'll discuss further below, went through some major changes based on feedback from the first pre-release. I spent a lot of time pondering how best to implement stickers, and with all the changes I needed to make in the last couple weeks to get it ready for the public release, things certainly didn't go as smoothly as usual. That, combined with other responsibilities and traveling to visit family over the last weekend, led to more delays than I was hoping for. I think things ended up in a pretty good place however, even if there are a few loose ends that I didn't have time to tie up, but I opted to get the update out now instead of delaying it further.

Stickers
Version 0.13 is all about stickers! These can be purchased in the form of sticker packs from Johnny's store. Once you have some stickers, you can apply them to the back of your You Can Think Pad by using a Packet-O-Matic, which you'll find in Johnny's Store and the Pit Stop. Once applied, stickers activate unique, game changing effects! For example, the backpack sticker will add another slot to your inventory, the YTP Multiplier sticker will increase earned YTPs by 25%, the Cash Register sticker will cause Johnny's Store to generate in a level, etc.



You can apply up to four stickers at once, and if you have multiple of the same sticker, its effects will stack! Whether you try to have a variety of different stickers or use a bunch of the same one to amplify its effects is up to you.

Normally, stickers will only last until you beat a level. However, if you'd like to keep your stickers longer, you can purchase glue sticks! Glue sticks can be used on sticker slots to make them extra sticky, which will hold the otherwise cheap, not very sticky stickers for an entire run. If you lose a level, your stickers will remain, but you'll have the opportunity to change things up if you'd like to change your strategy.

Lastly, know that once a sticker is applied, it cannot be removed without replacing it with another sticker, and when it's replaced it will be lost, so manage your stickers wisely, especially if you've used a glue stick!

With this first version there are 18 stickers available, with plans for more in future updates! I hope you all have fun discovering what they all do and how they can be used to your advantage!

What's next?
As I mentioned earlier, 0.13 still has a few loose ends to tie up, so I will try to chip away at some issues over the coming weeks. Some of these issues include:
  • The tutorial doesn't cover stickers yet. This will be a high priority for me... Once my voice recovers from a cold I got last week. I can't record new lines sounding like I do at the moment!
  • The Packet-O-Matic sprite is outdated considering it isn't used to open packets anymore. That will likely be changed at some point.
  • All sticker packs currently look the same. I will look into giving the different types different appearances so they can be differentiated without needing to read the descriptions.
  • Stickers can become hard to look through when you have a lot in your inventory. I will look into ways to improve this soon.
  • Currently it is not possible to view your applied stickers at any point during the game. This is something I'll add eventually.
  • The safe room is still using placeholder textures. These will be improved at some point. For now enjoy the copious amount of funny orange rugs.

Changelog
Changes marked with (Pre-release 2) primarily pertain to changes made between pre-release 2 and the public release.

Additions
  • Added stickers
    • Stickers can be purchased via sticker packs in Johnny's store. There are 18 stickers available as of this update.
    • Up to four stickers can be applied to the back of the You Can Think Pad at a time via the Packet-O-Matic, a new device that appears in Johnny's Store and the Pit Stop.
    • Each sticker activates a unique effect when applied.
    • When multiple of the same sticker are applied, their effects accumulate.
    • Applied stickers only last until a level is beaten.
    • Stickers can be made to last as long as the player wants by upgrading slots with the new Glue Stick item.
    • Applied stickers will be lost when replacing them with other stickers.
  • Updated the layout and design of Johnny's Store to account for stickers.
  • Added new entries into the credits.
  • Added diamond YTP pickups worth 250 YTPs. These are available to collect after winning field trip minigames.

Changes
  • Student Shuffle crowds now push entities at 95% of their movement speed instead of 100%.
  • Students now start out rewarding 100 YTPs when returning lost items instead of 75.
  • (Pre-release 2) Removed the stealth sticker from endless mode due to a crazy powerful exploit it enabled.

Bug Fixes
  • Fixed the inventory shrinking when full with the shrinking inventory challenge enabled when using quarters on vending machines or in other scenarios where using an item instantly replaces it with another.
  • (Pre-release 2) Fixed a typo in one of the sticker descriptions.
  • (Pre-release 2) Fixed the Fresh Sticker Pack not working when the player had at least one of every sticker in their possession.
  • (Pre-release 2) Fixed sticker pack descriptions not reappearing after restocking them.
  • (Pre-release 2) Blocked off all the walls in Johnny's store to prevent portal posters from being placed on them.
  • (Pre-release 2) Fixed captions appearing when sound effects for the sticker pack opening animation played.
  • (Pre-release 2) Fixed Bonus Sticker Packs being available in Endless Mode.
  • (Pre-release 2) Fixed the inventory shrinking even when full when entering a level after losing or replacing a Backpack sticker.

That covers everything about this update. I hope you all enjoy playing Baldi's Basics Plus version 0.13!

news.title: DELTARUNE Patch 1.04

news.description:
The wait is over, Punks: THE END is now live on Steam. And so marks THE END of our major updates for ANTONBLAST.

Of course, we may end up releasing some minor service updates as time goes on... but this is the final content update.

This update is, as of now, the closest thing to a definitive version of ANTONBLAST available. Our small team has been hard at work over the past months to put a neat bow on the game - addressing issues we had at launch, applying feedback we've received, and adding new content for good measure - and we think you'll really like the results.

Make no mistake, there's tons more we'd love to do... but that's probably best saved for another game, wouldn't you agree?

On a personal note: Thank you for over one year of support! This has been an incredible journey; making this game for you and sharing in the community was an experience beyond even our wildest imaginations. When I was first drawing Anton almost twenty years ago, I never could have conceived that he'd amass an audience of rabid punks excited to follow all of his weird adventures. You've made our dreams come true, that's for sure!

As for the future... All of us on the team can't wait to share what we're working on next. I can't give anything away today, but I think you'll be really surprised.

While this may be THE END of ANTONBLAST, it's only THE BEGINNING for SUMMIT.

Until next time, Punks...

Tony Grayson

Studio Head | SUMMIT

--

Patch Notes

(Sorry for the delay, Punks, this section takes time to write because it's a lot. - Alex)

NEW STUFF

  • Added the FOUR Lime Trials, in each wing of the casino: Shark Tank in the East Wing, Lime's a Beach in the West Wing, Distilled Hill in the Top Floor, and Rubble Rumble in Hell's Waiting Room. A reveal cutscene will play when you first enter the respective rooms.

  • The Battle Arena has finished construction and is now accessible at the far side of Peanut Park after you have defeated The Final Boss. In order to enter it and begin the Boss Rush, you must first acquire the Battle Pass from the Backstage area right after the entrance. There you will also find the new encounters with the Demon Corp: Kaz, Bonny, Emile and The Computer. Defeat them all and gain access to their final show, where you must face ALL FOUR at once!

  • COMPLETELY REVAMPED THE LAYOUT OF THE PAUSE AND SETTINGS MENUS. The whole thing's just different! Different background, typeface, options, etc.!

  • SEVEN NEW LANGUAGE OPTIONS are available in the settings menu. The game now has eight languages in total: English, French, Italian, German, Spanish, Portuguese, Latin-American Spanish and Brazillian Portuguese. These are courtesy of our community localizers, which you can now find in the staff roll.

  • FOUR new Achievements! Find the secrets of Peanut Park, chat up with Danton, win the Boss Rush and face all of the Lime Trials!

  • 17 NEW SPRAY CANS! One is a reward from completing the Boss Rush, nine are hidden throughout the casino, and four can be obtained in the Hallowed Halls (note: one of these is a replacement of a previous Spray Can). The rest are purchaseable at Brulo's Shop.

  • DANTON is now present throughout Brulo's Casino. Talk to him after a delightful interaction during a stage to listen to what rosy reaction you get out of him!

  • NEW DIFFICULTY MODES! Two new difficulty modes can be selected upon starting a new game. Hard Mode is unlocked after clearing Boiler City on any save file, whereas S. Hard Mode is unlocked after beating the game on Hard Mode. These challenges get EXTREMELY difficult, are you sure you're up for it? NOTE: These modes ONLY change the difficulty of the game. All achievements and unlocks can be obtained regardless of difficulty. There are no additional achievements or any of the sort on the harder difficulties, don't do them if you can't!

  • NEW GAME MODE! After you have beaten the game once, you can choose to play a new save file in Stage Rush Mode. This is the same game, but there is no Casino portion. As soon as you finish a stage, it's on to the next one! You will still visit Brulo's Shop inbetween stages, however. Go as fast as you can!

  • DEVELOPER TIMES for the Time Trials that can be seen after you have beaten the game. Can YOU do better than the makers? NOTE: Developer Times are only available in the Normal difficulty.

  • EXPERIMENTAL REPLAY SYSTEM so that you can save your best runs at a stage and play them back later! You can watch saved replays from the Main Menu. WARNING: This feature is not 100% reliable. This is an extremely difficult mechanic to nail completely. For best results, do NOT pause the game at any moment during a stage run that you will want to save a replay of. If you wish to helpfully report any desyncs, make sure to have a full recording of your gameplay footage alongside your replay.

Graphics

  • Changed the default text typeface to a larger, clearer font that also supports non-English latin characters. This also fixes the inconsistent line spacing.

  • Fixed menus being slightly darker than they should.

  • Fixed the Elevator static effect not using the center of the screen to determine which Elevator was closest to you.

  • Various tiling fixes across all stages and areas.

  • Spray Can splatters will now respect the transparency values of the color that is picked for the splatter.

  • Fix the Shutter Shades and Cowboy Hat flickering during the hitstop effect.

  • Fixed stray pixels on all pipe decoration tile sets.

  • Fixed anti-aliasing colors on all creeper decoration tile sets.

  • Fixed stray pixels on all dirt & mulch tile sets.

  • Fixed stray pixels on all metal & stone tile sets.

  • Fixed an issue with splatter effects being the wrong color.

  • Massively reduced the number of particles emitted from breaking crates.

  • Fixed the follower player (unlocked after beating the game) sometimes not having the correct palette.

  • Fixed Annie's clutch sprite being one pixel off the ground.

  • Fixed camera rotation persisting across room transitions.

  • Centered the smack death animation (the default one) to the screen.

  • Fixed centering of game area when the game is at a resolution that is not in a 16x9 aspect ratio.

  • Fixed the animations for the following backers' character in Peanut Park:

    • Cuthbert Bucket

    • robohobo93

    • Jamesharknado

Gameplay and UI

  • REMOVED the maximum HP limitation during Combo Chain mode. The description of the mode has been adjusted to match. However, CRACKED status will void if you ever reach 3 HP below your maximum! (Basically, if you would have otherwise died without the extra permanent HP.) The Golden Beet will no longer void it either.

  • Added a Random Spray Can option to the Spray Can selection. When selected, you will be assigned a random Spray Can from ones you own after every room transition.

  • Changed the default bind for Hammer Vault for new players to O on keyboard and R1/RB on controller. This should have only applied to new players, but it appears to also apply to any players who have not touched the input settings before. Sorry 'bout that... can only be fixed by going into the past now!

  • Reduced excessive screenshake from various sources.

  • Added a few more unique messages to the Destruction Meter payout.

  • Fixed not being able to Hammer Vault a second time if you did it and then grabbed a ladder.

  • Fixed not snapping to the floor immediately after going up a ladder, which would cause you to unintentionally Hammer Bounce off of it if you wanted to Clutch.

  • Improved Pinball ramp detection slightly.

  • When a Bert eats you, you will no longer be knocked back. Instead, you will remain in the Bert's mouth and be spat out after it is done eating.

  • Adjusted the hitboxes for the Angel reaction to match the character visuals.

  • Adjusted the hitbox of the Blobs.

  • Disabled dynamic difficulty if you have already cleared a stage or boss before.

  • The Health counter now flips the cards correctly, in a way that matches the Heartbeets you acquire. Previously it always and only ever flipped the card corresponding to your highest health point.

  • Exiting a sub-menu now remembers the index of the option you had selected previously.

  • The name of the boss or Lime Trial that is currently being fought now displays where the room name normally would in the Pause menu.

  • Fixed the name of certain Cassette Tapes being too long to fit on-screen. The name now has a rolling ticker effect.

  • Fluffed up the Cassette Tape menu a bit!

  • Fixed the description of Spray Cans appearing behind the characters.

  • Adjusted the visuals for the buttons on newly-acquired and currently-equipped Spray Cans.

  • Fixed the Stage Select menu causing the current room to completely reload.

  • Changed the Spray Can rewarded for the following goals:

    • Beat all 12 Par Times: Awards the Platinum Spray Can instead of the Golden Spray Can.

    • Clear all 12 stages in Combo Chain mode: Awards the CRACKED Spray Can instead of the Platinum Spray Can.

    • Find all 12 of Annie's Trash: Awards the Golden Spray Can instead of the Nothing Spray Can.

    • The Nothing Spray Can is now purchaseable at Brulo's Shop.

  • Added Cheat Codes.

Sound

  • Updated all music to use the newer Soundtrack versions.

  • Adjusted the mixing and volume of every sound effect, fixing clipping and drowning issues with various objects. sounds and music.

  • Fixed a few objects not having positional audio.

  • Added sound effects to various things in the game, such as purchasing items or growing vines. Too many to list here!

Brulo's Casino

  • Adjusted position of cardboard boxes in the Hallowed Halls

  • The Storage rooms of the Hallowed Halls are no longer pitch-black. They are now dimly-lit.

  • Three new Hallowed Halls Storage Rooms have been added, with the following unlock requirements:

    • Record every entry in the Exterminator's Handbook.

    • Interact with Bebo at all of his locations.

    • Purchase all of the trinkets from Brulo's Shop.

  • Shortened the intro sequence with Brulo by about a second.

  • Added twelve new Spray Cans to collect, peppered around the area. These include rewards for completing the Boss Rush as well as secret areas.

  • Repositioned various Fogs of War to prevent out of bounds clipping and other collision bugs.

  • Nina now has more dialogue that better reflects her mood and temper.

  • Fixed the text for the Spray Cans referring to them as "spraycan" as well as missing quotes.

  • The name of the Spray Can or Cassette Tape you are about to purchase now displays in the shop item name.

  • Purchasing a Spray Can now automatically equips it.

  • Added the Virtual Visor, Game Brick and Pet Paint to Brulo's Shop.

  • You can now purchase up to 20 balloons from Brulo's Shop, each costing more than the previous.

  • Adjusted the price of some shop items slightly.

  • Fixed invisible platforms in the secret entrances in the West Wing and Top Floor rooms.

  • Removed the default cassette tape from the Casette Menu, as it is no longer necessary.

  • Rewrote all of the dialogue from the Brulo-Egg devices to be helpful tutorial information, mostly.

  • Reorganized Hell's Waiting Room, removing the fog of war in the middle of the room in place of a mo

  • Fixed some parts of Skeletons in the Closet requiring unintended mechanics.

  • Made the "reward" for Skeletons in the Closet more obviously useless.

  • Muted intrusive Micro-Demons.

  • Added a new secret area. Legends tell that Brulo has stashed away things forgotten long ago in a secluded, protected zone in one of his establishments...

Boiler City

  • Removed the fade-to-black when the intro cutscene ends.

Slowroast Sewer

  • Fixed softlock issues in the room "Water Displacency"

  • Fixed the Cassette Tape not actually unlocking anything. It now unlocks the accidentally-previously-unused "Wildcard" cassette.

  • Fixed the transparency of the flowing water sprite.

  • Fixed a missing room name.

Brawlbuster

  • Lowered the intensity of the flickering contrast effect.

Cinnamon Springs

  • Renamed room "Or Swallow" to "Spitz Spitter Spittin'"

  • Enlarged the entrance to the side room "Asinine Aqueduct" from the "Cinnamon Spin" room.

  • Rotating platform arrays now have 3 platforms instead of 2. Additionally, they will now always be carried by Micro-Demons.

  • Removed intrusive Micro-Demons.

Bomb Candy Mines

  • Fixed stalactites not falling down if you left the room before they landed.

  • Changed a dynamic difficulty -exclusive checkpoint in the room "Diamond in the Rough" to no longer be dynamic difficulty -exclusive.

  • Shortened the gap in the background layer of the room "GIANT ENEMY CRAB!!" to make the jump back easier if all crates were broken.

  • Fixed bomb rocks sometimes exploding but not breaking anything.

Jewel Ghoul

  • Fixed being able to go out of bounds by continuously jumping on the Highball Rollers.

  • Fixed alignment of background visuals during the first phase.

  • Fixed cigarette sprites not being randomized during the second phase.

The Big Bath

  • Cassette Tape now unlocks the "Rain" cassette.

Concrete Jungle

  • Fixed chips in the room "Sawblade Sanctum" not being connected to platforms correctly.

  • Corrected the room name for "Mz. Keeter's Break Room"

  • Removed intrusive Micro-Demons.

  • Cassette Tape now unlocks the "Jungle Jazz" cassette.

Pinball Mire

  • Fixed certain objects not having the correct fade-in effect during the stage intro sequence.

  • Removed the grip effect during the Pinball reaction while you are spindashing on or over a paddle.

  • Fixed being able to enter or exit a Pinball Dispenser from the opposite side.

  • Fixed falling through the floor sometimes at the start of the room "The Great Excave"

  • Danton now shows up inside the Pinball Dispenser you leave him in.

Freako Dragon

  • Changed the logic for the positioning of the cracks in the third phase.

The Mad Mall

  • Fixed a missing room name.

  • The music for the heist portion of the stage will now always play from the beginning.

Crimson Factory

  • Fixed the final gate in the room "Sawdust Speedway" being able to close twice and look strange.

  • Cassette Tape now unlocks the "Gnome Encounter" cassette.

  • Fixed the Jetpack after-images not having the correct palette.

  • Fixed the Jetpack aiming the wrong away after being kicked off Anton/Annie.

Smallbuster

  • Slightly reduced the length of the various between-phase intervals.

  • Reduced the length of her final phase.

The Mysterious Glasshouse

  • Extended a ladder in the room "Moisture Maze"

  • Fixed the moving blocks in "Moisture Maze" having incorrect sprites.

  • Adjusted the graphics for the impenetrable valve door in the room "The Impenetrable Valve Door" to clarify level design intent.

  • Renamed the room "Spade and Neutered" to "Faint of Heart"

  • Added Brulo Chips to the room "The Lanes Between"

  • Added extra breakables to the room "The Impenetrable Valve Door"

  • Made the secret roof in the room "Angel Tower" slightly easier to squeeze into.

  • Fixed collision softlock in the room "Up and Down and All Around"

Maulbuster

  • Completely overhauled all of her bullet patterns. The patterns picked are now randomized, and they each last the same time according to their difficulty (all Easy patterns last the same time as each other, all Medium patterns last the same time as each other, all Hard patterns last the same time as each other).

  • Increased enemy spawn cooldown.

Ring-a-Ding

  • Adjusted anti-aliasing and contrast of background clock towers.

  • Extended the ending cutscene.

  • Increased the explosion radius of the Hate Meteors.

  • Fixed wind visuals not matching gameplay during the second phase.

Devilled Gardens

  • Removed a couple of broken Micro-Demons.

Hell Manor

  • Reworked the layout of the bottom half of the room "Pinballs to the Wall" to make the contest winner graffiti more visible.

  • Fixed softlock if you did an Antomic Blast into the toilet in the room "Hot Tub Hell Machine"

  • Fixed the color of chains in the room "Satan's Sewer"

The Final Boss

  • You can now skip the intro sequence.

  • Fixed Satan being one pixel off the ground during the intro sequence.

  • Flute summon attack now adds a flute solo to the current music.

  • Fixed straw sucking animations for Anton/Annie.

  • Minor miscellaneous adjustments to the timings of certain attacks during the fourth (Giga-Satan, regular) phase.

  • Corrected Anton's clothing in one of the shots during the ending cutscene.

  • Improved some of the lip syncing of Anton and Annie in the ending cutscene.

Miscellaneous

  • Renamed Annie's Spray Can "Dad's Suit" to "Iron Fist" and updated its description. The Spray Can itself is unchanged.

  • Fixed the tutorial for turning into a Bowling Ball always referring to Anton.

  • Slightly sped up the results menu sequence.

  • Various performance improvements across the whole game and a few more lag-prone sections.

  • Layers jumped are now tracked at Nina's.

  • Fixed incorrect names in the staff roll and backer credits.

  • Fixed settings not saving after exiting the initial boot settings menu.

  • Removed the "input map" setting because it did nothing.

  • Added a basic loading screen on boot.

  • Updated underlying GameMaker engine.

news.description:
The road has been long, the turns unforgiving… and now, only four teams remain.

On November 1st, the world’s fastest drivers will face off live from Paris Games Week, at the Dôme de Paris, for the ultimate title: Trackmania World Champions 2025.

Get ready for the speed, the stakes, and the spectacle of the year.

The Final Format

With a 3 hour window ; the bracket is simple, but merciless :

  • Semifinals: Best-of-5 matches

  • Grand Final: Best-of-7, winner takes all

The Final Four

  • Team Jetlag: Bren, Mudda managed by Wirtual. A unique mix of France, Australia, and Norway, ready to soar higher than ever in this tournament.

  • Team Nemesis: Mime and Puzstitopako are determined to silence the doubters and prove they belong on this stage.

  • Team BIG: Consistent, cold-blooded, and always clutch. Massa & Granady are hungry for victory.

  • Solary: The fan-favorite duo reborn under a legendary banner ! CarlJr & Pac return to the spotlight, this time representing Solary.

The Puffbros legacy lives on ; now flying the Solary flag. Expect the same passion, the same chemistry, and the same electrifying energy as they take on the grandest stage of them all.

Bracket & Schedule

  • The first Semi-final will be: BIG vs Jetlag

  • And the second one will be: Solary vs Nemesis

November 1st : Dôme de Paris, Paris Games Week

  • 10:30 CET: Opening Show & Semifinals

  • ~12:00 CET: Grand Final

  • ~13:00 CET: World Champion Ceremony

A €18,000 prize pool and eternal glory are at stake.

Where to what it ?

Watch the Finals Live on these Official Streams :

English Cast, with Turbo and NuaLaoch:

https://www.twitch.tv/trackmania

https://www.youtube.com/@TrackmaniaWorldTour/streams

French Cast, with Lutti and TMGGeek: 

https://www.twitch.tv/mgg_fr

Co-Streams:

Follow your favorite creators for alternative coverage (and a more biased one …) – including Wirtual, Spammiej, TrilluXe, Solary!

See You at the Finish Line

Whether you’re in the crowd at the Dôme or watching from home, don’t blink … you might miss the winning drift.

Make some noise for your contenders for the Trackmania World Cup 2025

The Trackmania World Cup 2025 Finals are set to make history.

Presented by Ubisoft and Webedia.

news.description:
Winter Wonderland
Mischief & Magic Mode

Now onto the part of the season that jingles, sparkles, and occasionally bursts into confetti. Winter Wonderland is back from December 9 – January 7 with Mischief & Magic, the beloved prop hunt game mode, on a familiar winterized map. For the first two weeks you will hide as props or hunt down your disguised foes with as much holiday spirit as you can muster while firing a weapon indoors (*please don’t actually fire weapons inside).

Halfway through the event, the magic shifts. Props wake up, stretch their little limbs, and decide they have had enough. Mischief & Magic Wonderland breathes life into your disguises, letting you fight back with explosive charm. Gingerbread bites. Snowmen detonate. The holiday spirit positively combusts. Through it all you can earn Frozen Demon Junkrat and Frozen Mountaineer Cassidy, track your progress in the Event Hub, and enjoy holiday chaos with friends who definitely did not see that candy cane coming.

Winter Wonderland Event 

This year also introduces a festive three-path reward system that lets you chase the cosmetics that speak to your style. Complete challenges across Winter Wonderland and other modes to earn Winter Cookies, then spend them on three tracks: Jingle Tracer, Krampus Junkrat, and Santa Claus Torbjorn. Each path has its own unique rewards, milestones, and personality to let you earn at your own pace and build that warm fuzzy sense of holiday joy while you play.

Collect your Cookies and bring some cozy winter fun to your fights December 9 through January 7.
news.title: Winter Wonderland 2025

news.description:
Lock & Load is back. The shooting range is officially open again to put your PG skills to the test once again. Gear up, line up the shot, and don’t blink.

This time, the rules have changed. The event runs longer now, so you’ve got room to breathe. But ammo won’t come for free. Want bullets? Complete personal tasks and frag opponents. Simple as that.

Hit targets, clear zones, stack guaranteed rewards with solid resources and loot. Consistency matters. Smart decisions matter more. That’s how shooters turn into sharpshooters.

Want the full Sharpshooter drip? Hat, mask, cloak, boots, plus the exclusive Sharpshooter skin are waiting inside the closed range with the Supergame Pass.

Lock & Load is back. Miss once — that’s on you.

executables.name: the jackbox party pack 11/the jackbox party pack 11.exe

news.description:
CAW CAW! BAGOODA! WEESNAW! These are the noises I make when I play Hear Say. I would never be able to make these noises if it weren’t for the genius Jackbox Games developers behind the project.

These outside-the-box thinkers created a new game for Party Pack 11 where you use your real phone microphone to make real noises that are put into the real game! We recently spoke to the team about what went into making Hear Say.

You can play Hear Say in The Jackbox Party Pack 11.

 

Please introduce yourself and your role on Hear Say.

Hi! I’m Alina Constantin, I was the game director for Hear Say.

I’m Bruno Rodríguez, and I worked on the art for Hear Say.

I’m Michael Siciliano, I programmed Hear Say.

I’m Avery Makel, I worked on the sound for Hear Say.

 

How did you come up with the idea for Hear Say?

Hear Say’s game idea started in Jackbox’s sound department. A group of sound designers and engineers prototyped a concept where players competed as foley artists, using microphone input to make silly sound effects for old movies. We built on that to go beyond literal dubbing and answer the question - how many different ways can we bring your sounds to life? -  Alina

Something the team added after the initial pitch was the intermission games (Ups & Downs, Nibble Noises, and Hup Hop). We did all this work to hook up microphones into the game and we really wanted to push what we could do with that beyond just recording words or sounds for a prompt. - Michael

 

Do you have any fun stories from making Hear Say?

Once people get comfortable recording, you get some spectacular nonsense.
When colleagues started to say that when they played, their pets were leaving the room or their partners checked in to see if they were ok, we knew we were getting there. 

One time we were testing out a music video idea, and got to hear each other belting insane lyrics we’d just made together. It wasn’t reliable enough to keep, but was a lot of fun - Alina

 

What makes the Hear Say dev team so special?

Everyone on the team is incredibly talented and dedicated to their work. Every detail of this game is meticulously crafted, tested and vetted on repeat; whether it’s camera motion, the impact of tied scores, the dynamic linework of an animated character, the exact audio range your phone is picking up and playing back, the exact sound effect or adjective to help players performances be fun, varied, and the joke land twice, amplified onto the exact split second off a patchwork of corporate footage that was definitely filmed with something else in mind… It’s very intentional and elaborate work to make something feel beautifully stupid at its best - Alina

There are no big egos, everyone offers their take on things but we respect each other a lot. I really enjoy what everyone worked on, from the hilarious prompts and videos that Paris \[Newton, [i]Hear Say’s [/i]Editorial Lead,] and Alina wrote and curated to the cool tech stuff Chase \[McClure] and Michael worked on for the controller and the game respectively. Avery \[Makel, [i]Hear Say’s [/i]Audio Lead,] made some incredible music, and Dutch \[Freese, our QA Lead’s,] playtesting of the game was thoughtful and his recorded sounds were all winners. I think we just had a really good combination of folks for Hear Say and we had fun making the game. - Bruno

 

What was one challenge you had to overcome during production?

One of the hardest nuts to crack were the final round clips. We wanted them to be a fun video backdrop for the player's individual sounds to make sense coming together, be surprising even as they’d already been replayed, yet still feel like their own. From old movie scenes to original trailers or commercial shorts, we tried a lot of things. We needed to build a modular film editing pipeline from scratch on top of a game relying on the nuances of microphone input, both of which we hadn’t done before. Landing on little bite sized narratives with the right mix of surreal and relatable took curation with a lot of iteration between 3 different departments. - Alina

I wanted the environment to feel more immersive, like entering a mysterious tunnel inside a TV. We’ve done similar things before with video but I wanted to make it more dynamic and lively so I asked Michael (our gameplay engineer) to help me make it real. We made a series of randomized panels that cycle through back and forth creating the illusion of motion in three dimensions. It took some elbow grease to make it work but we did! - Bruno

The infinitely-zooming panels might just be the most difficult set of animations I’ve worked on. Lots of considerations with aesthetics but also with performance. They look really good though! - Michael

 

What were you inspired by while designing the look and music for Hear Say?

We knew we’d be combining very different kinds of media between different player sound performance to different visual sources. A visual and sound personality that could both unify and harmonize it, while always spotlighting our players was important to me. Things like the 90s MTV idents were a nice inspiration for our tv world and how we framed things together - Alina

The main inspiration for the art was poster design from the 60s and 70s. Specifically Cuban movie posters, or American designers like Milton Glaser. They had this loose and playful feel to them. I think a reaction to more traditional realistic illustrations from the time that people would have seen everywhere. I also looked at odd musical instruments and devices as well as sciency visual representations of waveforms. - Bruno

 

What is your favorite part of Hear Say? 

So much of it brings up joy in different ways that it’s really hard to say. I’m proudest of the final round because it felt like the biggest challenge. But right now after spending months on hundreds of video files, my favorite parts to watch and play are the lobby and the blooper reel, the bookends outside the game. People making conversation in unexpected ways through the game; rerecording avatar sounds responding to each other, then nagging or highlighting each other with particular replays of their performances at the end, that really makes me happy - Alina

Art-wise maybe the game font, called Meltaface. It was the first time I tried creating a complete font and it was a somewhat difficult but rewarding process. I’m happy with the end result and I think it fits the game well, sometimes it is hard to find an existing font that does that. 
Here are some fun hidden symbols I made for the font. - Bruno

 

Any fun glitches or unexpected gameplay moments that happened during production and testing?

One time we had a playtester dive face first into a bean bag so others wouldn’t hear him recording. Another time at a DePaul campus playtest, one of the students recorded hiding behind a projector screen. A friend recorded their cat’s sounds, and stole a round with just that. Then there was that bug when I was trying to trigger specific prompts and got stuck staring into the beauty of our endlessly looping tunnel instead. - Alina

Just recently we finished a playtest and were trying to get notes on the prompts people saw. But any time someone tried giving their feedback, players would play a blooper video of an explosion with the sound of a wet fart. We never got to the notes. - Michael

 

What has been your favorite memory of making Hear Say?

My favorite memory is seeing one of our senior producers muted on camera, making silly faces and secret hand gestures with his son to record sounds together, and win the game. Having someone who’d actively participated since our earliest tests stating he knew he’d never be good at it, now be celebrated in-game while sharing this personal moment with his family and the team…absolutely golden. - Alina

Playing the game during our PAX West panel with a huge audience all playing the intermission games. Absolute pandemonium. - Michael
